contract_conformance.notes: §4.4 precondition table verified by an exhaustive phase × position × energy matrix (SLEEP gated to bed+night incl. the v1.2 energy-0-at-bed rule; HARVEST rejected at every pre-ready stage for all 3 crops; BUY/SELL shop-tile + gold/holdings gates; TALK_TO Chebyshev-1). Kickoff 9-step mock ladder, v1.2 constants (200g/5 seeds/energy table/prices), turnId event chains (turn_start→llm_call→action_chosen→action_resolved), ring cap 1000, and budget rule 5 (manager ceiling + per-agent 429 fallback) all conform. The two rule-1 violations found in the first QE pass (NaN qty economy corruption; rejecting-router loop death) were fixed in 89ccf81 and re-verified: the executor now enforces finiteness + whole-number >= 1 on qty and finite coords, and a throwing router degrades the turn to WAIT with a complete event chain.

test_coverage.notes: 267 tests across 26 files, all passing, zero skips. Builders shipped 169 honest tests plus new hardening regressions alongside the fixes; QE adds 85 adversarial tests in tests/qe/**: full SLEEP gate matrix, per-stage HARVEST rejection, BUY/SELL boundary/hostile-qty attacks (NaN/Infinity/fractional/negative now all REJECTED), 500-op economy fuzz with exact gold/item conservation ledger, 8-agent burst proving in-flight cap == 3, manager UTC-day budget reset + re-arm (badge exactly once per day), pause/step edge semantics, hostile parse battery (100KB garbage, multi-object, fences-in-strings preserved verbatim, unicode, prototype pollution, 10K nesting), throw-proof mockRouter under partial/hostile observations, multi-day replay determinism, and the kickoff gate run headlessly. Not covered headlessly (out of scope, browser verifier owns it): WorldScene/UIScene rendering, live Express endpoint integration (unit-level redact/budget/upstream mapping is covered).

reliability.notes: Headless gate run: 3 agents survive 5+ in-game days under fake timers with zero exceptions, gold strictly changes, every turnId chain complete, day_advanced ≥ 5, energy/gold/inventory invariants sampled throughout and never violated. Both latent reliability holes from the first QE pass are closed and regression-tested: a rejecting Router is converted to error-LlmResponse semantics ('router_threw:') so the turn degrades to WAIT and the agent loop keeps deciding (verified mid-run meltdown recovery + manager-level agent_error catch-all, 89ccf81); mockRouter — the budget-fallback safety net — is now throw-proof via normalizeObservation and degrades unreadable/partial observations to a deterministic WAIT (3b91777).

security.notes: Built bundle (dist/assets/index-*.js, 1.7MB) grep-verified clean: zero hits for 'freellmapi-', 'FREELLMAPI_API_KEY', ':3001', '127.0.0.1', 'Bearer <token>', or sk-/gsk_/AIza key shapes. Key lives only in server/.env (gitignored; only .env.example files tracked, both with empty key). src/ has zero key references; client talks only to the relative /api proxy path. Server error sanitization verified honest under attack: redaction runs BEFORE the 240-char cap (no partial-key survival at the truncation boundary), catches keys inside URLs, JSON blobs, Authorization echoes, and multiple mixed-vendor keys per message; budget 429 path returns the sanitized envelope without calling upstream. Model-output injection is contained by parse.ts (action whitelist, target shape+finiteness checks, no prototype pollution) AND now independently by the executor's mirrored gates (defense-in-depth, 89ccf81).

code_quality.notes: Strict TS clean (tsc --noEmit, noUnusedLocals/Parameters), contracts imported via @contracts alias with no divergent redeclarations, seams honored (world owns tiles, executor owns effects, agents are data), thorough intent comments. The first-pass dings are resolved: the executor's target guards now include the finiteness checks (gateQty centralizes the qty rule, 89ccf81) and the fence-stripping mutation is gone — extractFirstJsonObject is string-aware and preserves backticks inside JSON values (3b91777). Remaining polish item: single 1.7MB bundle chunk (Phaser), tracked as a MINOR issue.

issues.title: Client bundle is a single 1.7MB chunk
issues.detail: npm run build emits dist/assets/index-*.js at ~1,702.91 kB (gzip ~391.64 kB) with Rollup's >500kB warning — Phaser is bundled whole. Not a gate concern for a local demo; consider manualChunks if it ever ships.

resolved.title: Executor accepted BUY/SELL with qty:NaN and corrupted the economy to NaN
resolved.detail: src/agents/ActionExecutor.ts: isItemTarget/isVec2 now mirror parse.ts finiteness and gateQty enforces whole-number >= 1 (fractional qty is rejected outright instead of floored). Re-verified by tests/qe/executor-matrix.test.ts ('hostile non-finite quantities' — un-skipped and passing; fractional-qty test inverted to expect rejection with the 'whole number >= 1' reason) and the economy fuzz.

resolved.title: A rejecting Router killed that agent's loop forever via an unhandled rejection
resolved.detail: src/agents/AgentRuntime.ts wraps the router call in try/catch and converts rejections to error-LlmResponse semantics ('router_threw:'), so the turn degrades to WAIT with a complete turnId chain; src/agents/AgentManager.ts adds a loop catch-all that emits agent_error and lets the agent retry after cooldown. Re-verified by tests/qe/scheduler-adversarial.test.ts ('hostile router that REJECTS' — un-skipped, plus a new mid-run meltdown recovery test proving the loop keeps deciding).

resolved.title: mockRouter threw TypeError on a parseable-but-partial observation
resolved.detail: src/llm/mock.ts: normalizeObservation coerces any parsed object into a safe Observation (missing fields default conservatively), decide() is belt-and-braces wrapped, and unreadable input degrades to a deterministic 'observation unreadable' WAIT. Re-verified by tests/qe/mock-determinism.test.ts ('an observation missing fields does not throw' — un-skipped, plus a hostile-shape battery: arrays, nulls, wrong-typed fields all resolve).

resolved.title: stripFences mutated backtick fences inside JSON string values
resolved.detail: src/llm/parse.ts: the global fence pre-strip is removed; the string-aware brace scan skips fence markers naturally (they live outside the object) and preserves backtick sequences inside thought/say verbatim. Re-verified by tests/qe/parse-hostile.test.ts (thought now asserted byte-identical including the ```json fence text).

gate_decision.reason: The kickoff 'single hard requirement' is proven headlessly: 3 persona agents on mockRouter run till→plant→water→sleep→harvest/sell across ≥5 in-game days with gold strictly changing, zero exceptions, complete turnId event chains, and day_advanced ≥ 5. Both MAJOR defense-in-depth findings from the first QE pass (NaN qty economy corruption, rejecting-router loop death) and both MINOR ones (mockRouter throw on partial observation, fence-strip string mutation) are fixed in 89ccf81/3b91777 and re-verified by un-skipped adversarial regressions. Full suite 267/267 passing with ZERO skips across 26 files, typecheck clean, no API key or upstream URL in the client bundle, error redaction honest under adversarial input. The only open issue is a cosmetic bundle-size warning. Gate: proceed.
